title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
summary: Cole Medin discusses Google's whitepaper on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model accounts for only 10% of software development results while the harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) accounts for 90%. The video frames agentic engineering as the successor to vibe coding and emphasizes context engineering and orchestration skills as critical for 2026.
writeup: Cole Medin breaks down Google's whitepaper on agentic engineering, centering on the claim that the AI model drives only 10% of software development results while the surrounding harness, including context, tools, guardrails, and verification, drives the other 90%. The video positions agentic engineering as the successor to vibe coding and argues that context engineering and orchestration are the skills that will matter in 2026. The takeaway: results come less from picking a model and more from building the structure around it.
